_meta.label: TLA Stats — our own architecture
_meta.summary: How TLA Stats sources, computes, and verifies its numbers. Lets a reader (or on-site agent) answer 'where does this number come from and how is it verified?' the same way they'd ask about any protocol. source_type 'self' = our own method.

facts.claim: TLA Stats data is organized in layers: address-catalog (WHO — wallets, members, contracts), token-catalog (WHAT/WORTH — token identity + four-source pricing + LST redemption), and downstream crons for DEX, staking, and member data. Shared data lives in the tla-core repo under module/product/files.

Token prices come from four sources captured in one coherent snapshot: TLA (Eris backend /prices), CoinGecko, Astroport (tokens.byChain), and SkeletonSwap (warlock pools, pair-implied). We show all four plus a confidence/spread score rather than collapsing to one number.

All price sources and LST hub ratios are fetched in the same tight batch so they describe the same instant. Comparisons across sources captured at different times are invalid — this snapshot-coherence rule is why prices and ratios are captured together, not separately.
facts.notes: Demonstrated: an ampLUNA gap of 8% in one snapshot was 0.5% the next — timing, not a real divergence. Coherence + a 10% review threshold prevents false alarms.

facts.claim: For LSTs, the on-chain hub exchange rate gives the robust redemption price (base_price × ratio). The market/pool price is a weaker cross-check that can legitimately differ. We present both and only flag a large (>10%) gap for review — we never auto-correct or hide a source.

LP and ampLP positions are valued by share fraction (staked_shares / total_shares × pool_usd) from on-chain pool reserves — NOT amount × per-unit price. The per-unit model is provably wrong for these (confirmed by UI-vs-calc mismatches and by the ampLP fixed-supply/rising-backing design).
facts.notes: Mechanism sourced to Eris Amp Compounder (amplp.valuation_rule); the share-fraction implementation is ours.

facts.claim: Before a new cron replaces a legacy one, both run in parallel and their outputs are diffed; the new cron must match or beat the old on every shared number before the old is retired. token-catalog matched legacy LST ratios exactly (10 decimals) and prices 11/12 within 3% (the 12th being arbLUNA, where the difference is which source is headline, not a disagreement).

Core principle: honest data over false positives. We never ship a number that can't be sourced to chain truth, never silently correct a source we disagree with, and surface disagreement (with its size) rather than hiding it. Overrides live in curated config with notes, never invented into cron output.

Dashboard tiles are live feeds (RPC/chain primary) with the cron as fallback and historical capture only. Tile freshness is never tied to the cron's hourly cadence — live-primary, cron-fallback, snapshot last-resort.
